- Foetal Lobulation of Kidney

# Foetal Lobulation of Kidney

![](https://www.monash.edu/__data/assets/image/0006/1751901/OA1.jpg)

## Clinical History

The specimen was an incidental finding at autopsy.

## Pathology

The specimen is an adult kidney, stripped of its capsule and mounted to display the external surface. Slight grooves, which are regularly spaced, are visible on the surface of the kidney. This is an example of foetal lobulation.

## Note

The foetal kidney is composed of five lobules. Failure of complete fusion results in persisting foetal lobulation. This is a common finding of no clinical significance.
